name: Gödel First Incompleteness — Proof Graph
description: Diagonalization builds a sentence G that asserts its own unprovability. Structurally this parallels Cantor diagonal arguments: both encode "apply a transformation derived from a listing to obtain an object off the list / off the naive predicate".
mermaid:
flowchart TD
  T["Target: Gödel first incompleteness for consistent recursive T extending PA"] --> G["Given: arithmetization of syntax in T"]
  T --> A["Assumption: T is consistent and strong enough for representability"]
  L1["Lemma: provability predicate Bew is Sigma1 definable"] --> R["Assertion: recursive sets are representable in T"]
  G --> L1
  N["Construction: fix Gödel numbering of terms formulas and proofs"] --> D["Construction: diagonal lemma yields fixed-point sentence G"]
  R --> N
  D --> P["Algorithm capsule: build G by primitive recursive self-substitution on Bew negation code"]
  P --> E["Assertion: G is equivalent in T to formal unprovability of G"]
  E --> F["Assertion: if T proves G then standard arithmetic is inconsistent with truth of G"]
  F --> Q["Contradiction: T consistency forbids T proving G"]
  Q --> U["Assertion: G is not provable in T"]
  U --> W["Assertion: under soundness G holds in the standard model"]
  W --> K["Conclusion: T cannot decide every arithmetic sentence"]
